title: Narrow-Depth Secretary Cabinet with Mail Slots
summary: A tall, shallow face-framed cabinet with dividers forming mail sorting slots near the top and a euro-hinge flip-down secretary desk below — a genuinely narrow-depth design for a hallway or entryway.
description:
This cabinet is built genuinely narrow front-to-back, sized to fit a hallway rather than take up real room depth, and combines two distinct storage functions: mail sorting slots near the top and a flip-down secretary desk surface below. **Build the divider area first, before the shelves are attached to the sides** — the plan is specific that reaching in to add dividers after the box is closed up is a real problem, so the mail slot dividers get nailed to their shelf before that assembly ever meets the sides.

Once the divider-and-shelf subassembly is ready, attach all the shelves to the sides, using a shelf pin jig specifically for the top shelf and any shelves behind the lower cupboard doors so those stay adjustable. Check the whole case for square before the back goes on — the 1/4" plywood back is what a racked case reveals immediately, since it needs to lie flat across the entire rear opening.

The face frame is built as its own separate flat assembly, then attached to the front of the finished cabinet — keeping this step separate from the divider and shelf work is what makes it possible to build the face frame perfectly flat and square on a bench. Doors come last: the plan's own build used shop-made 1x3 frame-and-panel doors with leftover 1/4" plywood as the panel material, cut to leave about 1/8" clearance on all sides for the hinges, with one set of doors using euro-style hinges specifically for the flip-down secretary desk section.

tools.note: Ripping plywood if used instead of 1x12 stock.

materials.name: 1x12, 8 ft, or 3/4" plywood ripped to 11-1/4" strips
Euro-style inset hinges, for flip-down secretary door
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 1x12, 8 ft, or 3/4" plywood ripped to 11-1/4" strips
1x12, 8 ft, or 3/4" plywood ripped to 11-1/4" strips

cutList.note: Cut to fit each door opening.
Sized from leftover back panel material.

steps.title: Build the mail slot divider area
steps.body: Build the divider area first since it's difficult to add dividers after the box is assembled. Nail the divider boards to their shelf. If using pocket holes, drill first on the ends of the shelves.
materials: 1x12, 8 ft, or 3/4" plywood ripped to 11-1/4" strips

steps.title: Attach the shelves to the sides
steps.body: Attach all shelves, including the divider subassembly, to the sides. Use a shelf pin jig for the top shelf and any shelves behind cupboard doors so they stay adjustable.
materials: 1x12, 8 ft, or 3/4" plywood ripped to 11-1/4" strips

steps.title: Check square and attach the back
steps.body: Check the whole cabinet for square, then attach the back with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue to all fixed shelves.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame separately on a flat surface, then attach the completed face frame to the front of the cabinet.

steps.title: Build and fit the doors
steps.body: Build the doors with 1x3 frames and 1/4" plywood panels, cutting each door to fit its opening with about 1/8" clearance on all sides for the hinges.

steps.title: Install hinges and finish
steps.body: Install the upper and lower cabinet doors with inset hinges, and the secretary desk door with euro-style inset hinges. Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Euro-style inset hinges, for flip-down secretary door
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Narrow-depth secretary cabinet with mail sorting slots and a flip-down desk
